evidence: SQL_FIRST correctness/sql/api/answer/tool-call metrics are stable across current reruns and fair comparison.
Prior SQL_FIRST runtime was about 0.642s; current SQL_FIRST runtime ranged from 2.5058s to 3.6795s in comparable strict runs.
Final score moved with runtime/efficiency while correctness dimensions remained unchanged.
Candidate and SQL_FIRST are behavior-identical under current code, with candidate only faster in the fair comparison.

token_reduction.diagnostic_method: ENABLE_OFFICIAL_TOKEN_REDUCTION=0 strict run; this disables the full token-reduction feature, not only warning preservation
token_reduction.interpretation: disabling the full token-reduction feature changes answer/token behavior and is not evidence that warning preservation caused drift; warning preservation itself is trajectory/readiness metadata and should be kept
